title: In Vitro Transcription of DNA Oligos by T7 Polymerase
author: Audrey Lapinaite
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ssqeedw

steps.instruction: Mix the following in a final volume of 100 µl: 20 µl 5x transcription buffer 1.5 nmol T7oligo (15 uM final) 1.0 nmol template (10 uM final) DEPC H 2 O to 100 µl 5x transcription buffer Contributed by users 1.5 nmol T7oligo Contributed by users 1.0 nmol template Contributed by users DEPC H2O Contributed by users
Heat at 70-80 °C for 2 minutes. Undetermined, Heating 00:02:00, Heating
Slow cool on bench-top.

Use immediately or store at -20 °C. For some applications, prior gel purification of the DNA template may be desirable. However, for my transcriptions, the oligos are not very long and I immediately gel purify the RNAs after transcription, so purity of the DNA oligo is not a major concern. Undetermined, Storage
Add 500 μl of RNase-free H 2 O to the 70-80 mg of each NTP. undetermined, RNase-free H2O

Bring pH of each NTP to 6.8-7.2 by adding 5 µl of 5N NaOH followed by 10-90 µl of 1N NaOH. Monitor pH in the stripes with 6.5-10 range. Most adjustment will be needed for ATP, least - for UTP. 5N NaOH Contributed by users 1N NaOH Contributed by users

Measure absorbance of 500 fold dilution: 3 µl to 1.5 ml of H 2 O. ATP λ max 259 nm, ε max 1.59 × 10 4 cm -1 M -1 CTP λ max 271 nm, ε max 0.9 × 10 4 cm -1 M -1 GTP λ max 253 nm, ε max 1.37 × 10 4 cm -1 M -1 UTP λ max 259 nm, ε max 1.59 × 10 4 cm -1 M -1
Calculate concentrations and required dilutions to reach 100 mM concentration each.

Mix the following in a 100 μl reaction: %s %s My typical final yields on transcription reactions of this size are roughly 30 μl of 10-20 uM (300-600 pmol). For my biochemical purposes, this is more than enough material. However, the above reaction can easily be scaled up. undetermined, 100 mM ATP undetermined, 100 mM CTP undetermined, 100 mM GTP undetermined, 100 mM UTP undetermined, 1M DTT undetermined, 1 mg/mL pyrophosphatase (Roche) undetermined, 1 mg/mL T7 polymerase
Incubate at 37 °C, overnight. Undetermined, Incubation 16:00:00, overnight incubation

DNase treat: Add 5 units (5 μl) RNase-free DNase directly to reaction. Incubate at 37 °C, 30 minutes. RNase-free DNase Promega undetermined, RNase-free DNase Undetermined, Incubation 00:30:00, Incubation This step is optional and may be omitted.

Add equal volume (100 μl) gel loading buffer directly to transcription reaction. undetermined, Gel loading buffer My RNAs run very close to the xylene cyanol dye. Therefore, I use a gel loading buffer that contains only bromophenol blue.

Gel-purify transcribed RNAs on a medium thickness, denaturing urea-polyacrylamide gel. For my RNAs (~30 nt) I use a 15% gel. At this polyacrylamide concentration, with RNA from a 100 μl transcription reaction, I get excellent separation of the desired RNA length from n+1 and n-1 contaminants (these arise from template-independent addition of nts by T7 polymerase; and synthetic template oligos that are not the full length). Optimization of the gel running time and acrylamide concentration will be necessary for gel-purifying RNAs of different length.

PAGE Fragment Resolution: Denaturing Conditions (6M Urea) a Clean glass plates with EtOH and coat with 200 μl Silanization Solution I. EtOH Contributed by users Silanization Solution I Contributed by users undetermined, Silanization Solution I
Assemble glass plates.
Prepare 15% urea polyacrylamide gel. 15% urea polyacrylamide gel Contributed by users

Denaturing Urea Polyacrylamide Gel (300 ml for a gig gel: 35 x 25 cm) %s %s After addition of APS and TEMED to Urea-Polyacrylamide mixture pour the gel immediately. 40% Acrylamide-bisacrylamide (19:1) Contributed by users Urea Contributed by users Water Contributed by users 10% APS Contributed by users
Place comb and polymerize for 60 min. 01:00:00, Polymerize
Assemble electrophoresis apparatus.
Remove comb.
Fill inner and outer buffer chambers with 1x TBE. 1x TBE Contributed by users
Rinse wells with 1x TBE buffer. 1x TBE buffer Contributed by users
Pre-run the gel for 30 min to heat-up and remove remaining urea. Set constant P=40 W, 3000 V, 400 mA. 00:30:00, Pre-running gel
Load sample.
Run P=40 W, 3000 V, 400 mA for around 3 h, until the bromophenol blue band is migrating in the last 1⁄4 of the gel. 03:00:00, Running gel
Remove one of the glass plates and cover the gel with saran wrap. Flip. Remove another glass plate and cover all gel with saran wrap.
UV shadow band and excise with sterile razor and place in Falcon tube.
Break gel slices with p-1000 tip.
Crush and soak gel slices in equal volume of RNase-free water. RNase-free water Contributed by users
Elute RNA overnight by rocking at 4 °C. Undetermined, Eluting RNA

Filter out gel pieces by spinning sample through Corning Spin-X filter tube. Centrifuge at top speed for 5 minutes, 4 °C. Undetermined, Centrifugation 00:05:00, Centrifugation I cut the top off a P-1000 tip so that I can pipette as much as possible of the water-gel slurry into the Corning filter tube. Corning Spin-X filter tube Contributed by users

Add 30 μl 10x NEBuffer 3 to each sample, then 1 μl CIP (10 U/μl). Incubate at 37 °C for one hour. 10x NEBuffer 3 Contributed by users undetermined, 10x NEBuffer 3 CIP (10 U/μl) New England Biolabs undetermined, CIP Undetermined, Incubation 01:00:00, Incubation This step is only required if removal of the 5’ triphosphate is required (e.g. if the RNA will subsequently be 5’-radiolabeled with T4 PNK.)
Phenol/chloroform extract with 300-500 μl phenol-chloroform solution, pH 8.0. Phenol-chloroform solution Contributed by users undetermined, Phenol-chloroform solution
Ethanol precipitate the RNA, by adding 1/10 volume 3 M NaOAc (pH 5.2), 1/100 volume 100x linearized acrylamide and 3 volumes 100% ethanol. [described in step 35-40 below]
Incubate at -80 °C, at least 1 hour Undetermined, Incubation 01:00:00, Incubation
Spin down at top speed, 20 minutes, 4 °C. Undetermined, Spinning down 00:20:00, Spinning down
Remove supernatant.
Wash pellet with 70% ethanol.
Spin down again and remove supernatant.
Dry pellets in speed vacuum, ~10 minutes. 00:10:00, Drying pellets
Resuspend pellets in 30 μl DEPC H 2 O (or buffer) and store at –20 °C. undetermined, DEPC H2O (or buffer) Undetermined, Storage
